summary: Alfred codebase is very clean with minimal dead code. All major modules are actively used (panel system, rainbow_csv, etc.). No orphaned files detected. No zombie dependencies found. Test code is properly contained in mod test blocks. Code after returns is rare and intentional. Zero permanent commented-out code blocks found. Only minor issues: one possible unused import path in tests, and some verbose test helper code.

unused_exports.name: Right (PanelPosition variant)
unused_exports.reason: Defined in PanelPosition enum but never constructed or pattern-matched in any crate. Left/Bottom/Top are used in renderer.rs; Right is not.
unused_exports.action: Safe to remove; architectural decision to omit right-side panels

suspicious_patterns.pattern: Module imported in lib.rs but only called from Lisp bridge
suspicious_patterns.notes: rainbow_csv module is accessed only via Lisp bridge (colorize_buffer is registered as a primitive). Direct Rust usage is via tests. This is intentional plugin-driven architecture.

All 15 register_*_primitives functions are called from alfred-bin main.rs. Each registers a group of Lisp primitives. Some primitives may be unused by plugins, but the registration functions themselves are all used.
All 12 helper functions are called from render_frame. No dead helpers detected.

test_code_assessment.notes: All tests are in mod test blocks (not at file scope). No test-only functions left in public API. E2E tests use pexpect for deterministic terminal interactions. Test code is well-contained and removable.

import_analysis.module_isolation: Excellent: clear dependency graph with no cycles

plugin_code_assessment.notes: All 8 plugins are loaded at startup: vim-keybindings (128 LOC), browse-mode (281 LOC), rainbow-csv (51 LOC), status-bar (33 LOC), line-numbers (30 LOC), default-theme (28 LOC), word-count (21 LOC), test-plugin (5 LOC). test-plugin is intentional example; no stale plugins.

recommendations.title: Remove unused PanelPosition::Right variant
recommendations.description: The Right variant is defined in crates/alfred-core/src/panel.rs but never constructed or pattern-matched. Remove it to simplify the enum and clarify supported panel positions (Top, Bottom, Left only).
recommendations.impact: Code clarity; unused enum variant is confusing to readers

recommendations.title: Add clippy::dead_code checks to CI
recommendations.description: Run clippy with -W warnings to catch any new dead code before merge. This is mostly already done (lint target) but ensure --all-targets is used.
recommendations.impact: Prevents accumulation of dead code; catches unused variants and functions

recommendations.title: Document why some Lisp primitives are not used by plugins
recommendations.description: Some registered primitives (e.g., panel-entry-count, panel-cursor-line) are defined but not called by any plugin. Add comments explaining if they are for future use or can be removed.
recommendations.impact: Clarifies intent; prevents future developers from wondering about unused primitives
